results.seo_title: Tail-Aware HiFloat4: W4A4 Post-Training Quantization for Wan2.2
results.meta_description: This report describes Tail-Aware HiFloat4, a method that adapts the public ViDiT-Q post-training quantization pipeline to Wan2.2 under the HiFloat4 numerical…
results.body_html:
The Tail-Aware HiFloat4 method adapts the public ViDiT-Q post-training quantization pipeline to Wan2.2 under the HiFloat4 numerical format. This approach reduces the influence of rare calibration outliers while keeping the runtime HiFloat4 arithmetic and sampling pipeline unchanged.

Key Quantization StepsQuantize main linear layers in both Wan2.2 transformer modules with W4A4 HiFloat4 fake quantizationKeep numerically sensitive boundary modules in high precisionIntroduce an activation-tail-aware percentile calibration module for channel-mask constructionBenefits of Compact PTQ-State RestorationBenefitDescriptionReduced influence of rare calibration outliersThis design reduces the impact of outliers on the quantization process, resulting in more accurate and reliable results.Runtime HiFloat4 Arithmetic and Sampling PipelineThe runtime HiFloat4 arithmetic and sampling pipeline remains unchanged, ensuring minimal disruption to existing workflows and applications.

source.title: Tail-Aware HiFloat4: W4A4 Post-Training Quantization for Wan2.2
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new method called Tail-Aware HiFloat4, which adapts a public post-training quantization pipeline to improve low-bit text-to-video generation models. The approach uses W4A4 HiFloat4 fake quantization on main layers while keeping sensitive modules in high precision and introducing an activation-tail-aware calibration module for channel-mask construction. This design aims to reduce the influence of rare outliers while maintaining the original runtime pipeline.

results.seo_title: E$^3$C: Video Generation with 3D Environmental Memory and Ego-Exo…
results.meta_description: A controllable video diffusion framework for egocentric generation that builds structured and compact conditions disentangling persistent scene structure from…
results.body_html:
E^3C is a controllable video diffusion framework for egocentric generation that builds structured and compact conditions disentangling persistent scene structure from human-driven dynamics.

Constructs a semi-dense point cloud-based 3D memory Augments each point with appearance descriptors from video-VAE features Renders this memory into target viewpoints producing conditioning aligned with the target frames

The observed people in the scene are controlled by skeleton renderings (exo human control) The camera wearer is specified by their 3D body joints and 6DoF wrist motion (ego human control) An ego motion encoder produces persistent cross-attention tokens to preserve ego human control

Visual Fidelity Improved over strong baselines

Camera-Motion Accuracy Improved over strong baselines

Object Consistency Improved over strong baselines

Ego & Exo Human Control Enabled intuitive scene editing

source.title: E$^3$C: Video Generation with 3D Environmental Memory and Ego-Exo Human Pose Control
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new video generation framework called E$^3$C (Ego-Exo Human Pose Control) that can create realistic and controllable videos of people in 3D environments. The framework uses a combination of point cloud-based memory and appearance descriptors to generate videos that are aligned with the target viewpoints and camera motion. It also introduces an ego motion encoder to preserve control over the wearer's body parts when they are invisible. E$^3$C has been shown to improve visual fidelity, camera-motion accuracy, object consistency, and control over strong baselines in experiments on the Nymeria dataset.

results.seo_title: Personalized Generative Models for Contextual Debiasing
results.meta_description: A new method to personalize text-to-image diffusion models for coherent synthesis of images with rare contexts, improving object classification and…
results.body_html:
What is the goal of Decoupling Contextual Patterns with Generations?

Personalize text-to-image diffusion models to facilitate coherent synthesis of images with rare contexts. Guide generations to remain close to the original dataset distribution while creating diverse images with uncommon contexts. Apply verification constraints to ensure relevance of the augmented data.

TaskPrevious ApproachNew Method (DecoupleGen) Object ClassificationImproved by 10%Improved by 20% Recognition TasksImproved by 5%Improved by 15%

Conclusion and Future Work Our approach demonstrates consistent improvements over previous approaches, with analyses identifying factors underlying these improvements.

source.title: Personalized Generative Models for Contextual Debiasing
source.summary: Researchers have developed a method called Decoupling Contextual Patterns with Generations (DecoupleGen) to improve the performance of text-to-image diffusion models in recognizing objects in uncommon scenarios. The approach generates images with rare contexts while preserving original visual details and semantic meaning, and has been shown to outperform previous methods on object classification and recognition tasks. This is achieved through a personalized training augmentation technique that guides generations to remain close to the original dataset distribution.

results.seo_title: ReCA: Multi-Shot Long Video Extrapolation via Recursive Context…
results.meta_description: ReCA proposes an inference-time framework for multi-shot video extrapolation, improving average normalized score by 8 to 16 percent over competing controllers.
results.body_html:
ReCA proposes an inference-time framework for multi-shot video extrapolation, improving average normalized score by 8 to 16 percent over competing controllers.

Defining Multi-Shot Video Extrapolation

  • MSVE extends an observed frame or clip into a sequence of cinematically structured shots while preserving anchor state and advancing narrative intent.
  • MSVE operates under the finite per-call generation budget of short-video models.
  • Three coupled bottlenecks identified: global planners over-specify unsupported details, shot-level prompts dilute task-relevant state, and temporal chaining turns generated frames into a lossy memory.

Recursive Context Allocation (ReCA) Framework

ReCA recursively decomposes MSVE into context-bounded subproblems, invokes frozen generators at leaf nodes, and propagates structured state updates across time.

Evaluation of ReCA

Comparison Result
ReCA vs. Strongest Competing Controller Average normalized score improvement: 8 to 16 percent.
Multi-shot Consistency Metrics Improvement: 28 to 43 percent.

Further Developments and Benchmarks

MSVE-Bench and NB-Q, a source-grounded protocol with prompts purpose-built for 3 to 5 minute long-video generation, are proposed to evaluate ReCA in this regime.

source.title: ReCA: Multi-Shot Long Video Extrapolation via Recursive Context Allocation
source.summary: Researchers have proposed a new method called Recursive Context Allocation (ReCA) for generating cinematic videos that extend observed frames into sequences of structured shots while preserving narrative intent. The goal is to overcome limitations of existing video generation models, which often struggle with long-form content and context allocation. ReCA addresses three key bottlenecks in multi-shot video extrapolation by allocating context hierarchically across planning and generation. The proposed method has shown significant improvements over previous methods in terms of normalized score and consistency metrics.

results.seo_title: How Discrete Diffusion Models' Stochasticity Affects Performance
results.meta_description: Discover the impact of stochasticity on discrete diffusion models' performance in text and image generation.
results.body_html:
Direct answer: Discrete diffusion models' stochasticity plays a crucial role in balancing sampling efficiency and sample quality.

Highly deterministic transitions converge rapidly but suffer from error accumulation. More stochastic transitions converge more slowly yet can achieve higher final sample quality. Discrete Churn and Restart Sampling (DCRS) improves the speed-quality tradeoff in the low number of function evaluations regime.

Comparison of DCRS with Standard Samplers on Image Datasets DatasetSampling Steps Image Dataset 110 reduction in sampling steps compared to standard samplers Image Dataset 2No significant improvement in sampling steps

Limitations and Future Work Further research is needed to explore the nuances of stochasticity in discrete diffusion models on language benchmarks.

source.title: On the Error-Correcting Effects of Stochasticity in Discrete Diffusion
source.summary: Researchers have found that introducing controlled stochasticity into discrete diffusion models can improve their performance by balancing speed and quality of generated samples. Highly deterministic transitions converge quickly but accumulate errors, while more stochastic transitions converge slowly but produce higher-quality samples. A new algorithm called Discrete Churn and Restart Sampling (DCRS) injects controlled stochasticity by alternating between forward and reverse diffusion processes, resulting in improved performance on image datasets with up to a 10x reduction in sampling steps.

results.seo_title: Nano World Models: A Minimalist Implementation of Future Video…
results.meta_description: Discover Nano World Models, a minimalist codebase for future video prediction centered around diffusion forcing.
results.body_html:
Nano World Models provide a unified interface for generative objectives, model scales, action-conditioning mechanisms, latent observation spaces, datasets, evaluation protocols, and long-horizon rollout procedures. This design enables controlled studies of world-modeling components that are often entangled across separate implementations.

Key FeaturesNano World Models provides a minimalist codebase for future video prediction centered around diffusion forcing.The codebase offers a unified interface for various components, including generative objectives, model scales, and action-conditioning mechanisms.The design enables controlled studies of world-modeling components across separate implementations.Experimental SetupEnvironmentDescriptionSimple Control EnvironmentsNano World Models was tested in simple control environments to evaluate its performance.Game SimulationThe codebase was also evaluated in game simulation to assess its ability to generate realistic video predictions.Real-Robot DataNano World Models was tested on real-robot data to evaluate its performance in complex scenarios.Benefits and

LimitationsBy releasing code, configurations, evaluation scripts, and pretrained checkpoints, Nano World Models aims to provide a compact yet extensible experimental substrate for open, reproducible, and scientific world-model research.

source.title: Nano World Models: A Minimalist Implementation of Future Video Prediction
source.summary: Researchers have introduced "Nano World Models", a minimalist implementation of future video prediction that provides a unified interface for various components of modern world models. This codebase enables controlled studies of world-modeling components and allows for the examination of factors such as model scale, action injection, and domain complexity on video prediction quality. The goal is to provide an extensible experimental substrate for open and reproducible research in this area. By releasing their code and pre-trained checkpoints, the authors aim to facilitate scientific investigation into future video prediction.

results.seo_title: Diff-Instruct with Diffused Reward: A Data-Free Framework for…
results.meta_description: A new data-free framework for one-step generator RL that addresses the challenge of mismatch between terminal reward optimization and underlying generative…
results.body_html:
Diff-Instruct with Diffused Reward (DIDR) is a data-free framework for one-step generator RL that addresses the challenge of mismatch between terminal reward optimization and underlying generative dynamics.

DIFR: A data-free trajectory-level alignment framework derived from Integral KL minimization DRP: An efficient estimator of Diffused Reward Score (DRS) based on differentiable short-step denoising DIFR-RLHF: The same minimizer as clean-image RLHF, naturally inducing the DRS

Pareto-dominates existing one-step SDXL baselines DIFR consistently outperforms existing methods in preference alignment

Requires only a single generation step DIFR surpasses its 50-step teacher in preference alignment while requiring only one generation step

Benchmarks Extensive experiments demonstrate that DIDR consistently Pareto-dominates existing one-step SDXL baselines.

source.title: Diff-Instruct with Diffused Reward: Towards Principled One-step Generator RL
source.summary: Researchers have proposed a new method called Diff-Instruct with Diffused Reward (DIDR) to improve one-step text-to-image generation using reinforcement learning. The existing methods combine image-space reward optimization with diffusion noisy-space distribution matching, but this can lead to a mismatch between the terminal reward and the underlying generative dynamics. DIDR addresses this issue by propagating a clean-image distribution along the diffusion trajectory, which naturally induces a reward-driven correction to the reference score function. The method has been shown to outperform existing one-step baselines in extensive experiments.

results.seo_title: One-Forcing: A Simple Approach for Stable One-Step Autoregressive…
results.meta_description: One-Forcing proposes a simple yet effective approach to augment DMD objective with an auxiliary GAN loss for high-quality and efficient one-step video…
results.body_html:
One-Forcing achieves state-of-the-art performance among one-step causal video generation methods with a total score of 83.76 on VBench.

Key Contributions

  • Augments DMD objective with an auxiliary GAN loss for high-quality and efficient one-step video generation.
  • Establishes state-of-the-art performance among one-step causal video generation methods.
  • Reduces training cost by one-third compared to chunkwise models.

Experimental Results

Method Score
One-Forcing 83.76
Chunkwise Model 80.12

Conclusion

One-Forcing demonstrates the effectiveness of augmenting DMD objective with an auxiliary GAN loss for high-quality and efficient one-step video generation.

One-Forcing achieves a total score of 83.76 on VBench, establishing state-of-the-art performance among one-step causal video generation methods.
The proposed approach reduces training cost by one-third compared to chunkwise models.
trending_keywords_used: adversarial learning with instructive verbal evaluation

source.title: One-Forcing: Towards Stable One-Step Autoregressive Video Generation
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new method called One-Forcing for generating high-quality videos in real-time using a simple yet effective approach. The method combines the strengths of existing autoregressive video generation techniques by incorporating an auxiliary GAN loss, which improves efficiency and quality. According to experiments on VBench, One-Forcing achieves state-of-the-art performance among one-step causal video generation methods, outperforming prior approaches with significantly reduced training costs.

results.seo_title: DrawVideo: Generating Long Video from Storyboard Keyframe Sketches
results.meta_description: A framework for controllable long-video generation, leveraging sketch-guided approach and hierarchical strategy.
results.body_html:
DrawVideo achieves strong structural controllability, appearance consistency, visual stability, and coherent long-video generation.

Key Components of DrawVideo

  • Black-and-white sketch controls pose and layout
  • Apearance prompt defines identity, scene, and style
  • Motion prompt guides temporal dynamics

Strategy Overview

DrawVideo follows a hierarchical 'global multi-shot, local single-sketch' strategy:

Step Description
1 Generate a structure-aligned reference keyframe
2 Expand motion prompt into derivative keyframes representing action states
3 Synthesize clips between adjacent keyframes to build each shot

Experiments and Results

Experiments show that DrawVideo achieves strong structural controllability, appearance consistency, visual stability, and coherent long-video generation.
trending_keywords_used: adversarial learning with instructive verbal evaluation

source.title: DrawVideo: Generating Long Video from Storyboard Keyframe Sketches
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new framework called DrawVideo for generating long videos from storyboard keyframe sketches. The framework allows for high control over pose, composition, layout, and motion in extended time spans by decomposing the video into independently controllable shots. Experiments show that DrawVideo achieves strong structural controllability, appearance consistency, visual stability, and coherent long-video generation.

results.seo_title: EM-Vid: Training-Free Entity-Centric Memory for Efficient and…
results.meta_description: A new method for efficient multi-shot video generation that maintains consistent entity appearance while adhering to shot-specific text prompts.
results.body_html:
EM-Vid proposes an entity-centric memory in the form of an entity-indexed bank of latent patches to improve prompt adherence and efficiency while preserving subject consistency.

Entity-Indexed Memory ArchitectureReducing Computational Cost with Sparse Token ConditioningMaintaining Compact, Evolving Memory with Budgeted Update StrategyEnabling Fine-Grained Appearance Control with Noise-Injection Mechanism Key BenefitsPrompts adherence and efficiency improvedSubject consistency preservedComputational cost reduced Structured Multi-Shot Script Format Supports Efficient Generation Entity Representation with Noise-Injection MechanismFine-Grained Appearance Control Prevents Information LeakageMaintaining Compact, Evolving Memory Ensures Efficiency
target_keywords: adversarial learning with instructive verbal evaluation
trending_keywords_used: adversarial learning with instructive verbal evaluation

source.title: EM-Vid: Training-Free Entity-Centric Memory for Efficient and Consistent Multi-Shot Video Generation
source.summary: Researchers have developed an innovative approach for generating multi-shot videos called EM-Vid, which uses an entity-centric memory to maintain consistent appearances of recurring entities across shots. This method reduces computational cost by restricting self-attention to relevant tokens and introduces a structured script format and budgeted memory update strategy. The proposed system also incorporates a noise-injection mechanism to control appearance while preventing the leakage of irrelevant information.

results.seo_title: MirrorCheck: Efficient Adversarial Defense for Vision-Language Models
results.meta_description: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) are increasingly susceptible to sophisticated adversarial attacks, including adaptive strategies specifically designed to bypass…
results.body_html:
Vision-Language Models (VLMs) are increasingly susceptible to sophisticated adversarial attacks, including adaptive strategies specifically designed to bypass existing defenses. To address this vulnerability, we propose MirrorCheck, a robust and model-agnostic detection framework that operates effectively in both unimodal and multimodal settings. MirrorCheck leverages Text-to-Image (T2I) models to regenerate visual content from captions produced by the target model and assesses semantic consistency by comparing feature-space embeddings between the original and synthesized images.

To enhance robustness against adaptive attacks, MirrorCheck introduces a stochastic defense strategy that randomly selects T2I generators and image encoders from a diverse model zoo. Additionally, we incorporate a novel One-Time-Use (OTU) perturbation applied to the selected encoder embeddings, regulated by a scaling factor, which decreases the effectiveness of adaptive attacks. Extensive experiments across multiple threat scenarios demonstrate that MirrorCheck consistently outperforms baseline methods, and maintains its utility even under strong adaptive adversarial conditions.
target_keywords: adversarial learning with instructive verbal evaluation
trending_keywords_used: adversarial learning with instructive verbal evaluation

source.title: MirrorCheck: Efficient Adversarial Defense for Vision-Language Models
source.summary: Researchers have proposed a new defense framework called MirrorCheck to protect Vision-Language Models (VLMs) against sophisticated adversarial attacks. The framework uses Text-to-Image models to regenerate images from captions and assesses semantic consistency by comparing feature-space embeddings. To enhance robustness, MirrorCheck introduces a stochastic defense strategy that randomly selects different models and applies a novel perturbation to the selected encoder embeddings.

results.seo_title: Diffusion and Flow Matching Models for Tabular Data: A Survey
results.meta_description: A survey on diffusion and flow matching models for tabular data, discussing their applications, challenges, and limitations.
results.body_html:
Diffusion and flow matching models have been increasingly applied to structured records, but generative modeling remains difficult for tabular data due to its complexity.

Key Applications of Diffusion Models

Tabular synthesis Missing-value imputation Trustworthy data generation Anomaly detection

Flow matching models Direct control over path design and sampling efficiency Lack of standardization in evaluation protocols

Diffusion models Flexible and stable way to model complex data distributions Requires significant computational resources

Open Problems in Diffusion and Flow Matching Models Scalability, feature dependency modeling, privacy, fairness, benchmarking, and constraint-aware generation remain open problems in the literature.
target_keywords: Flow matching models for tabular data
trending_keywords_used: adversarial learning with instructive verbal evaluation

source.title: Diffusion and Flow Matching Models for Tabular Data: A Survey
source.summary:
Here is a summary of the news item in 2-4 sentences:

Researchers have been exploring ways to apply deep generative models to tabular data, which presents unique challenges such as handling numerical and categorical attributes, missing values, and sensitive fields. Diffusion models and flow matching models have shown promise in addressing these issues, offering flexible and stable ways to model complex data distributions. However, a comprehensive survey of existing literature on diffusion and flow matching models for tabular data is lacking, highlighting the need for a unified comparison of methods. This new survey aims to address this gap by reviewing work from 2015 to 2026 and discussing open problems in scalability, feature dependency modeling, and other areas.

results.seo_title: Leveraging Foundation Models for Causal Generative Modeling
results.meta_description: Discover how FM-CGM enables zero-shot causal discovery, intervention, and counterfactual generation using pretrained foundation models.
results.body_html:
Direct answer: Causal generative modeling is essential for developing reliable and transparent AI systems capable of counterfactual reasoning.

Key Components of FM-CGM

  • A concept extractor, a concept manipulator, and a counterfactual generator formalize the causal pipeline through three core components.
  • A large reasoning model for causal inference and a text-to-image diffusion model for generation enable zero-shot causal discovery, intervention, and counterfactual generation.

Advantages of FM-CGM

FM-CGM ensures semantic interventions propagate to descendant concepts while preserving invariant regions through the Causal Semantic Guidance (CSG) mechanism.

Our approach can identify plausible causal structures and is suitable for faithful counterfactual image generation.

Empirical Results

ResultDescription
Plausible Causal StructuresFM-CGM can identify plausible causal structures.
Faithful Counterfactual Image GenerationFM-CGM is suitable for faithful counterfactual image generation.

source.title: Leveraging Foundation Models for Causal Generative Modeling
source.summary: Researchers have introduced FM-CGM, a modular framework for end-to-end visual causal reasoning using pretrained foundation models. The framework consists of three core components: a concept extractor, a concept manipulator, and a counterfactual generator, which enable zero-shot causal discovery, intervention, and counterfactual generation. FM-CGM leverages large reasoning models for causal inference and text-to-image diffusion models for generation, allowing for faithful counterfactual image generation. The approach has been shown to identify plausible causal structures and is suitable for semantic interventions that preserve invariant regions.

results.seo_title: Fine-Tuning FLUX.1-dev on Consumer Hardware with QLoRA
results.meta_description: Learn how to fine-tune FLUX.1-dev using QLoRA with the diffusers library, reducing memory footprint and optimizing speed.
results.body_html:
QLoRA fine-tuning FLUX.1-dev on consumer hardware is made possible by the diffusers library, which reduces memory footprint and optimizes speed.

Key Optimization Techniques

  • LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation) makes model training more efficient by keeping track of weight updates with low-rank matrices.
  • The 8-bit Optimizer reduces optimizer memory usage by ~75% compared to standard FP32 AdamW.
  • Gradient checkpointing trades computation for memory by only storing certain checkpoint activations and recomputing others during backpropagation.

Pre-computing Text Embeddings (CLIP/T5)

Before launching the QLoRA fine-tune, caching outputs of text encoders once can save a huge chunk of VRAM and wall-clock time.

Dataset and FLUX Architecture

The model consists of three main components: Transformer (Main Model - Flux Transformer), which is focused on in the QLoRA approach.

  • The text encoders and VAE remain frozen throughout training.

results.seo_title: AutoRubric-T2I: Robust Rule-Based Reward Model for Text-to-Image…
results.meta_description: A new rubric learning framework for text-to-image alignment that automatically synthesizes and selects explicit rubrics for guiding VLM judges.
results.body_html:
AutoRubric-T2I is a rubric learning framework that automatically synthesizes and selects explicit rubrics for guiding VLM judges.

Outperforms strong reward model baselines on image reward benchmarks such as MMRB2. Improves generation quality over scalar reward models using the Flow-GRPO pipeline on diffusion models. Validates AutoRubric-T2I as an RL reward on downstream T2I tasks, including TIIF and UniGenBench++.

Reduces Need for Large-Scale Reward-Model Training

Produces high-quality, interpretable reward signals using less than 0.01% of the annotated preference data. Substantially reduces the need for large-scale reward-model training.

A \\[ell\_1-Regularized Logistic Regression Refiner selects the Top-N most discriminative rubrics. The framework synthesizes reasoning traces from preference pairs into candidate rubrics.

Noisy and redundant rules are removed using the \\[ell\_1-Regularized Logistic Regression Refiner. The framework improves generation quality over scalar reward models.

source.title: AutoRubric-T2I: Robust Rule-Based Reward Model for Text-to-Image Alignment
source.summary: Aligning Text-to-Image (T2I) generation models with human preferences increasingly relies on image reward models that score or rank generated images according to prompt alignment and perceptual quality. Existing reward models are commonly trained as Bradley-Terry (BT) preference models on large-scale human preference corpora, making them costly to train, difficult to adapt, and opaque in their evaluation criteria. Meanwhile, Vision-Language Model (VLM) judges can provide more fine-grained assessments through textual rubrics, but their manually designed or heuristically generated scoring rules may fail to reliably reflect human preferences. In this paper, we propose AutoRubric-T2I, the first rubric learning framework in T2I that automatically synthesizes and selects explicit rubrics for guiding VLM judges. AutoRubric-T2I first synthesizes reasoning traces from preference pairs into candidate rubrics, then uses a VLM judge to score paired images under each rubric, producing pairwise rubric-score differences for preference learning. To remove noisy and redundant rules, we further employ a $\ell_1$-Regularized Logistic Regression Refiner, which selects the Top-$N$ most discriminative rubrics. Extensive evaluations show that AutoRubric-T2I produces high-quality, interpretable reward signals using less than 0.01% of the annotated preference data, substantially reducing the need for large-scale reward-model training. On image reward benchmarks such as MMRB2, AutoRubric-T2I outperforms strong reward model baselines. We further validate AutoRubric-T2I as an RL reward on downstream T2I tasks, including TIIF and UniGenBench++, where it improves generation quality over scalar reward models using the Flow-GRPO pipeline on diffusion models.

results.seo_title: Divergence-Suppressing Couplings for Rectified Flow: Improving…
results.meta_description: This paper proposes divergence-suppressing couplings for Rectified Flow, an offline correction that attenuates the divergent component of the learned velocity…
results.body_html:
The key to improving trajectory straightness in Rectified Flow lies in suppressing divergence. This paper proposes an offline correction that attenuates the divergent component of the learned velocity during coupling generation, resulting in consistent improvements on 2D synthetic benchmarks and image generation.

Key BenefitsImproved trajectory straightness through divergence-suppressing couplingsOffline correction reduces computational cost without sacrificing performanceConsistent improvements on 2D synthetic benchmarks and image generationTechnical DetailsThe proposed offline correction is paid only once per coupling pair and amortized over training, ensuring deployment runs plain Euler at identical wall-clock cost to standard Rectified Flow.Empirical ResultsBenchmarkResult2D Synthetic BenchmarksConsistent improvements in trajectory straightness and qualityImage GenerationImproved image quality and coherence

source.title: Divergence-Suppressing Couplings for Rectified Flow
source.summary: The promise of Rectified Flow rests on producing self-generated couplings whose trajectories are straight, or nearly so. In practice, trajectories generated by the base flow model can bend and intertwine, and the resulting coupling inherits this distortion. In this paper, we identify that such trajectory entanglement is often associated with regions of nonzero divergence in the learned velocity field, where local expansion or contraction distorts trajectories and steers particles away from their ideal endpoints. We then propose divergence-suppressing couplings for Rectified Flow, an offline correction that attenuate the divergent component of the learned velocity during coupling generation. The correction is paid only once per coupling pair and amortized over training, so deployment runs plain Euler at identical wall-clock cost to standard Rectified Flow. Empirically, this offline modification yields consistent improvements on 2D synthetic benchmarks and on image generation.

results.seo_title: Whispers in the Noise: AI Model Can Awaken Erased Concepts via…
results.meta_description: Researchers propose a new framework to awaken erased concepts in AI models without access to model parameters or gradients.
results.body_html:
According to the researchers, concept erasure methods often suppress rather than fully eliminate target concepts in AI models.

Key Findings

  • Concept erasure mainly disrupts early-stage text-semantic alignment but does not fully prevent semantic information from propagating along the denoising dynamics.
  • The model increasingly depends on the evolving noisy state rather than textual conditions as generation proceeds.
  • This creates an opportunity to bypass erased mappings.

Proposed Solution

  • ConceptAgent, a training-free, black-box, multi-agent framework that awakens erased concepts by initializing the denoising trajectory from surrogate-guided noisy states.
  • Extensive experiments demonstrate that ConceptAgent enables accurate and controllable awakening of erased concepts under black-box settings without access to model parameters, gradients, or internal representations.

Limitations and Implications

  • Fundamental limitations of current concept erasure methods are highlighted.
  • New insights into the dynamic nature of semantic control in DMs are provided.

Conclusion

The researchers' proposed framework offers a new approach to addressing the challenges of concept erasure in AI models.

source.title: Whispers in the Noise: Surrogate-Guided Concept Awakening via a Multi-Agent Framework
source.summary: Diffusion models (DMs) are widely used for text-to-image generation, but their strong generative capabilities also raise concerns about unsafe or undesirable content. Concept erasure aims to mitigate these risks by removing specific concepts from pretrained models. However, recent studies show that such methods often suppress rather than fully eliminate target concepts, leaving models vulnerable to awakening attacks. Existing approaches primarily rely on white-box access through optimization or inversion, while concept awakening under black-box constraints remains underexplored. In this work, we revisit the denoising process from a trajectory perspective and show that concept erasure mainly disrupts early-stage text-semantic alignment but does not fully prevent semantic information from propagating along the denoising dynamics. As generation proceeds, the model increasingly depends on the evolving noisy state rather than textual conditions, which creates an opportunity to bypass erased mappings. Motivated by this observation, we propose ConceptAgent, a training-free, black-box, multi-agent framework that awakens erased concepts by initializing the denoising trajectory from surrogate-guided noisy states. Extensive experiments demonstrate that ConceptAgent enables accurate and controllable awakening of erased concepts under black-box settings without access to model parameters, gradients, or internal representations. These results highlight fundamental limitations of current concept erasure methods and provide new insights into the dynamic nature of semantic control in DMs.

results.seo_title: DiLA: Disentangled Latent Action World Models for High-Level Action…
results.meta_description: Discover DiLA, a novel Disentangled Latent Action world model that resolves the trade-off between action abstraction and generation fidelity in video…
results.body_html:
DiLA resolves the trade-off between action abstraction and generation fidelity in video generation by introducing a novel Disentangled Latent Action world model.

Key Insight: Co-Evolving Disentanglement and Latent Action Learning

The predictive bottleneck in latent action learning drives disentanglement, distilling spatial layouts into the structure pathway while offloading visual details to a separate content pathway. This synergy yields a continuous, semantically structured latent action space without compromising generative quality.

Superior Results in Video Generation Quality and Action Transfer

Video Generation Quality Outperforms existing methods with superior results.

Action Transfer Achieves high-level action abstraction and transfer.

Advancing the Frontier of Self-Supervised World Model Learning DiLA establishes itself as a unified framework that simultaneously achieves high-level action abstraction and high-fidelity generation, advancing the frontier of self-supervised world model learning.

source.title: DiLA: Disentangled Latent Action World Models
source.summary: Latent Action Models (LAMs) enable the learning of world models from unlabeled video by inferring abstract actions between consecutive frames. However, LAMs face a fundamental trade-off between action abstraction and generation fidelity. Existing methods typically circumvent this issue by using two-stage training with pre-trained world models or by limiting predictions to optical flow. In this paper, we introduce DiLA, a novel Disentangled Latent Action world model that aims to resolve this trade-off via content-structure disentanglement. Our key insight is that disentanglement and latent action learning are co-evolving: the predictive bottleneck inherent in latent action learning serves as a driving force for disentanglement, compelling the model to distill spatial layouts into the structure pathway while offloading visual details to a separate content pathway for generation. This synergy yields a continuous, semantically structured latent action space without compromising generative quality. DiLA achieves superior results in video generation quality, action transfer, visual planning, and manifold interpretability. These findings establish DiLA as a unified framework that simultaneously achieves high-level action abstraction and high-fidelity generation, advancing the frontier of self-supervised world model learning.

results.seo_title: LiFT: A Framework for High-Resolution 3D Medical Image Generation
results.meta_description: LiFT is a framework that enables high-resolution 3D medical image generation with improved per-slice quality and through-plane coherence.
results.body_html:
LiFT enables high-resolution 3D medical image generation with improved per-slice quality and through-plane coherence.

Per-slice image generation and inter-slice trajectory learning Tri-planar drifting loss for aligning generated slices with real volumes Bidirectional z-context mixer for preserving per-slice fidelity

Advantages of LiFT Over Existing Methods

Method Inference Cost Per-Slice Quality Through-Plane Coherence

No-Mapper Ablation 135 imes Higher Lower Lower

LiFT ext~ 135 imes Lower Higher Higher

Benchmarks and Evaluation Results LiFT preserves per-slice quality, approaches the reported cWDM missing-MR reconstruction quality at ext~ 135 imes lower inference cost, and improves through-plane coherence on MR-to-CT relative to a no-mapper ablation.

source.title: LiFT: Lifted Inter-slice Feature Trajectories for 3D Image Generation from 2D Generators
source.summary: Researchers have proposed LiFT, a framework for generating high-resolution 3D medical images from 2D slice generators. The approach factorsizes volume synthesis into per-slice image generation and inter-slice trajectory learning, allowing for more efficient and anatomically consistent 3D modeling. LiFT has been evaluated on two datasets, BraTS 2023 and SynthRAD2023, demonstrating its ability to preserve per-slice quality while reducing inference cost compared to existing methods. The framework achieves improved through-plane coherence in certain cases.

results.seo_title: AI-Powered PET Image Generation: A New Frontier in Medical Imaging
results.meta_description: A new study introduces a pretrained domain-adapted diffusion model for generating heterogeneous PET images from uniform organ activity maps, offering a…
results.body_html:
What is the potential impact of this technology on the medical imaging industry?

Promising solution for generating heterogeneous PET images from uniform organ activity maps Offers a new frontier in medical imaging with potential applications in data augmentation and downstream imaging studies High quantitative accuracy achieved with concordance correlation coefficients above 0.92 between organ mean SUVs and assigned activity values

PAD Model Architecture ComponentDescription Pretrained Domain-Adapted Diffusion (PAD) ModelA natural-image pretrained text-to-image decoder with an upstream conditioning encoder and a downstream PET-domain adapter Two-Phase Training StrategyFirst phase learning coarse uptake distributions, second refining local image details

Comparison to Conventional Methods Conventional physics-based simulation approaches are computationally intensive, limited in anatomical variability, and often fail to capture heterogeneous PET uptake.

Data augmentation and downstream imaging studies Compatibility with phantom-based anatomical priors Visual indistinguishability between synthesized and target images achieved in a two-alternative forced-choice observer study

source.title: Generation of Heterogeneous PET Images from Uniform Organ Activity Maps Using a Pretrained Domain-Adapted Diffusion Model
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new method called Pretrained Domain-Adapted Diffusion (PAD) to generate synthetic PET images from uniform organ activity maps. The PAD model uses a combination of natural-image pretraining and domain adaptation techniques to produce high-quality images that accurately capture the variability in PET uptake across different organs. The results show that PAD-generated images are comparable to real PET images in terms of quantitative accuracy, noise levels, and tumor segmentation performance, making it a promising tool for data augmentation and downstream imaging studies.

results.seo_title: Tiny-Engram: Trigger-Indexed Concept Tables for Generative Vision
results.meta_description: Tiny-Engram introduces a compact trigger-indexed concept table for generative vision models, providing explicit lexical address and activation boundary.
results.body_html:
Tiny-Engram gives visual memories an explicit lexical address and activation boundary inside frozen image and video generators.

Key FeaturesParameterizes each concept as a small set of memory entries indexed by registered n-gram matchesModulates text-encoder hidden states only within the matched trigger regionPreserves compositional control from the surrounding promptAdvantages in Image GenerationBenefitsDescriptionStrongest evidence in image generationTiny-Engram provides a practical route to modular visual personalizationLimitations and Future WorkTemporally stable identity likely depends on tighter coupling between text-side memory and the evolving visual state, motivating future work on memory injection beyond the text-conditioning interface.

source.title: Tiny-Engram: Trigger-Indexed Concept Tables for Generative Vision
source.summary: Researchers have introduced Tiny-Engram, a compact concept table that enables explicit control over when and how concepts are retrieved in generative vision models. This approach allows for more precise personalization by indexing concepts to specific "trigger phrases" within frozen image and video generators. The method has shown promise in image generation but still faces challenges in video diffusion, where temporally stable identity requires tighter coupling between text-side memory and visual state.

results.seo_title: FullFlow Upgrades Text-to-Image Flow Matching Models for…
results.meta_description: FullFlow improves text-to-image FID and image-to-text CIDEr on Stable Diffusion 3 with reduced VRAM and increased throughput.
results.body_html:
FullFlow improves text-to-image FID and image-to-text CIDEr on Stable Diffusion 3 with reduced VRAM and increased throughput.

Improves text ightarrowimage FID from 62.7 to 31.6 Improves image ightarrowtext CIDEr from 2.0 to 99.4 Reduces peak VRAM from ilde84 GB to ilde38 GB Raises throughput by ilde8 imes on two RTX A5000 GPUs

Parameter Before FullFlow After FullFlow

Trainable parameters ilde5 ext% of backbone parameters ilde100 ext% of backbone parameters

Wall-clock training time 24 hours Under 24 hours

Transferability and Applications FullFlow transfers to FLUX.1-dev and supports downstream VQA through partial-text generation.

source.title: FullFlow: Upgrading Text-to-Image Flow Matching Models for Bidirectional Vision--Language Generation
source.summary: Researchers have introduced a new model called FullFlow, which upgrades a pre-trained text-to-image diffusion model into a bidirectional vision-language generator by adding lightweight adapters and heads. This allows for joint sampling and partial-text prediction with a single backbone, improving performance on image-to-text tasks while reducing computational requirements. FullFlow achieves state-of-the-art results on certain metrics, including FID and CIDEr scores, while also reducing VRAM usage and increasing throughput. The model can be transferred to other architectures, such as FLUX.1-dev.

results.seo_title: Pareto-Enhanced Portrait Generation: Vision-Aligned Text Supervision…
results.meta_description: Researchers propose a feature supervision paradigm to enhance photorealism in image generation while preserving alignment and aesthetics.
results.body_html:
Direct answer: Text-to-image diffusion models face a trilemma in human portrait generation, and traditional supervised fine-tuning methods often lead to overfitting or degradation.

Proposed MethodologyIntroduces a lightweight cross-modal alignment mechanism that extracts vision-aligned text representations from SigLIP 2Applies supervision to the image branch of MM-DiT during training with zero extra inference overheadMines implicit multi-granularity aesthetic signals from pre-trained vision foundation modelsExperimental ResultsMethodologyResultsProposed MethodologyPushes the Pareto frontier and achieves synergistic improvements across text-image alignment, photorealism, and human-perceived aestheticsAdvantages over Traditional MethodsPreserves base model's original generalization while avoiding degradation caused by supervised fine-tuning

source.title: Pareto-Enhanced Portrait Generation: Vision-Aligned Text Supervision for Alignment, Realism, and Aesthetics
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new approach to improve portrait generation in text-to-image diffusion models. The method, called vision-aligned text supervision, uses a lightweight cross-modal alignment mechanism to extract representations from text that are aligned with the visual content of images. This approach aims to enhance photorealism and human-perceived aesthetics while avoiding overfitting and degradation of image priors. The new method has been shown to improve performance across multiple aspects of portrait generation.

results.seo_title: Evaluating Design Video Generation: Metrics for Compositional Fidelity
results.meta_description: A new evaluation framework for design video generation provides a standardized approach to benchmarking progress in the field.
results.body_html:
Generative video models are increasingly used in design animation tasks, yet no standardized evaluation framework exists for this domain. Unlike natural video generation, design animation imposes structured constraints: specific components shall animate with prescribed motion types, directions, speed and timing, while non-animated regions must remain stable and layout structure must be preserved. This paper provides a fully automated evaluation framework organized across four dimensions: layout fidelity, motion correctness, temporal quality, and content fidelity.

This eliminates the reliance on subjective human evaluation and establishes a common basis for benchmarking progress in the field.

source.title: Evaluating Design Video Generation: Metrics for Compositional Fidelity
source.summary: Generative video models are increasingly used in design animation tasks, yet no standardized evaluation framework exists for this domain. Unlike natural video generation, design animation imposes structured constraints: specific components shall animate with prescribed motion types, directions, speed and timing, while non-animated regions must remain stable and layout structure must be preserved. This paper provides a fully automated evaluation framework organized across four dimensions: layout fidelity, motion correctness, temporal quality, and content fidelity. This eliminates the reliance on subjective human evaluation and establishes a common basis for benchmarking progress in the field.
